flashcards.front: What are the three types of System V IPC?
flashcards.back: Message Queues, Semaphores, and Shared Memory

flashcards.front: Which header file contains definitions for all System V IPC functions?
What is the purpose of the ftok() function?
flashcards.back: To generate a unique key for IPC objects based on a file path and project ID

flashcards.front: How do you remove an IPC object programmatically?
flashcards.back: Using the xxxctl() function with IPC_RMID command (e.g., msgctl, semctl, shmctl)

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of shared memory over other IPC mechanisms?
flashcards.back: It provides the fastest communication by allowing direct memory access without data copying
